summary: Stellar three‑set 1974 show captured on an excellent SBD transfer – flawless jams (Half Step, China > Rider, Truckin’ > Mind‑Left‑Body > Spanish) and a thunderous Eyes with superb mix.
review: The July 31 1974 Dillon Stadium concert is widely hailed as one of the peak ‘74 performances – three sprawling sets packed with extended jams, tight transitions and classic moments like Half Step, China > Rider, an epic Truckin’ suite and a soaring Eyes.  The Charlie Miller SBD transfer is praised for its crystal‑clear balance, deep drum mic’ing and minimal glitches, making the music sound as vivid as the original Wall of Sound experience.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews overwhelmingly positive (4‑5 stars) praise both the musical performance and the soundboard recording; only a single low‑star review mentions a missing track, so the combined show (70%) and recording (30%) quality yields a near‑top rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Charlie Miller’s SBD transfer is described as crisp, balanced and powerful. Minor glitches/drop‑outs are noted but do not detract from overall listenability; floor toms are highlighted for deep mic’ing.

standout_songs: Truckin' > Mind Left Body Jam > Spanish Jam
show_quality.setlist_flow: Three‑set marathon with fluid segues; first set builds, second set delivers marathon jams (notably a 30‑minute Truckin’ suite), third set caps the night with a relaxed yet still inspired finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for soaring tone, flawless solos and “flames” on China > Rider and the long Truckin’ jam.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass work highlighted as ‘killin’ it’, especially on Eyes; noted for tight interplay with Bill Kreutzmann.
band_member_comments.Bob: A few mentions of a low‑end note on Wharf Rat intro; generally not singled out for criticism or special praise.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s piano and Moog work lauded as a highlight of the Wall of Sound era, adding depth to jams.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming described as “perfect” and “awesome”, with floor toms captured in deep‑mic style.
